Tests that the logarithm of a negative number returns NaN using Math.log.

# Purpose
This code is a simple test script that uses an assertion to verify the behavior of the `Math.log` function in JavaScript. It checks that the logarithm of a negative number, specifically `-0.00001`, returns `NaN` (Not-a-Number), which is the expected result according to the IEEE 754 standard for floating-point arithmetic. The use of `assert` ensures that the test will fail if `Math.log(-0.00001)` does not return `NaN`, indicating a potential issue with the `Math.log` implementation. This script provides narrow functionality, focusing solely on validating a specific mathematical operation.
